rationale: All four events (A0, EV-001, EV-002, EV-003) are Windows Security event 5145 (network share object access) against SYSVOL, which is routine domain-joined behavior: EV-001 and EV-002 show host-01$ (the machine account) reading well-known default GPO gpt.ini files ({31B2F340...} and {6AC1786C...} are the Default Domain Policy and Default Domain Controllers Policy GUIDs) via a link-local IPv6 address, consistent with normal Group Policy refresh; A0 and EV-003 show user-02$ (another machine account from 10.0.1.4) enumerating the corp.local\Policies directory, also consistent with GPO processing. Access masks (0x120089 = read/synchronize, 0x100081 = read attributes/list directory) are read-only and non-destructive. No write, modify, or suspicious tool execution is evidenced, and no anomalous process or lateral-movement indicators are present in the package.
